goal: Replace selector-based report data paths with the query execution engine as sole data pipeline, driven by Query IR in Redux

seed_queries.unfiltered_transactions: Query with Identity computation, single Transactions source, groupBy 'category', no filters — must produce identical output to S.Transactions.tree
seed_queries.unfiltered_positions: Query with Identity computation, single Positions source, groupBy 'account', no filters — must match S.Positions.tree
seed_queries.large_transactions: And([GreaterThan('amount', 500)]) — transactions over $500, grouped by category. Selectors cannot do this.
seed_queries.dining_multi_account: And([Equals('category', 'Food:Dining'), In('account', ['Checking', 'Savings'])]) — dining at specific accounts
seed_queries.payee_pattern: Matches('payee', '^WAL') — payees starting with WAL (Claude regex path)
seed_queries.amount_range: And([Between('amount', 100, 1000), Or([Equals('category', 'Food'), Equals('category', 'Shopping')])]) — mid-range spending in two categories
seed_queries.inactive_accounts: LessThan('balance', 0) on Accounts domain — negative balance accounts

steps.action: Expand IRFilter type definition (type-definitions/ir/ir-filter.type.js) from 2 variants to 10: keep existing Equals and OlderThan, add In(field:[String], values:[String]), GreaterThan(field:String, value:Number), LessThan(field:String, value:Number), Between(field:String, low:Number, high:Number), Matches(field:String, pattern:String), And(filters:[IRFilter]), Or(filters:[IRFilter]), Not(filter:IRFilter). Spike learning: new leaf variants use String for field (not regex constraint) — enables filtering on enriched fields like categoryName. Combinators reference IRFilter recursively. Update IRSource filters field from '[IRFilter]' to 'IRFilter?' (optional single root node — And([...]) wraps multiple filters, undefined means no filter). Run `yarn types:generate-all`. Run style validator on type definition file — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: As planned. Also updated test fixtures in 4 test files for filters→filter rename.

steps.action: Write failing tests for IRFilter boolean tree evaluation. Test file: test/query-language/ir-filter-evaluation.tap.js. Tests for every leaf: Equals field match, In set membership, GreaterThan numeric comparison, LessThan numeric comparison, Between range inclusive, Matches regex pattern, OlderThan (existing). Tests for combinators: And([Equals, In]) both must pass; Or([Equals, Equals]) either passes; Not(Equals) inverts; nested And(Or(...), Not(...)) evaluates correctly. Guard tests: And([]) throws; Or([]) throws; depth > MAX_FILTER_DEPTH throws; Matches with invalid regex throws. Seed query tests: build the 'large_transactions' and 'amount_range' seed queries from the task's seed_queries field, verify they filter correctly against fixture entities.
steps.note: As planned. 38 tests covering all variants, combinators, seed patterns, and guards.

steps.action: Create ir-filter-evaluator.js in src/query-language/. Export a function that takes an IRFilter tree and returns a pre-compiled predicate `entity => Boolean`. Pre-compilation: In values converted to Set for O(1) lookup, Matches pattern compiled to RegExp once (wrapped in try/catch — throw with clear message on invalid regex), Between/GreaterThan/LessThan capture numeric thresholds. Recursive tree walks And/Or/Not. Include MAX_FILTER_DEPTH constant (20) — throw on exceeded depth. Reject empty And([])/Or([]) with clear error. Use P/T cohesion groups. Run style validator — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: File named build-filter-predicate.js per style validator (export must match file name with verb prefix). Uses T cohesion group only — no P needed.

steps.action: Make the failing ir-filter-evaluation tests pass via the evaluator. Run style validator on test file — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: As planned. All 38 tests pass.

steps.action: [CHECKPOINT] Run complexity review on query-execution-engine.js. Report style card violations found. Wait for approval before proceeding.
steps.note: Approved to proceed with step 6 refactoring.

steps.action: Refactor query-execution-engine.js to use ir-filter-evaluator. Spike learning: compound filters require enrich-then-filter (enriches ALL transactions, then applies evaluator) because predicates may reference enriched fields like categoryName. When source has no filter (undefined), skip filtering. When source has only simple Equals filters on raw fields (category, account, payee), the existing filter-then-enrich path can remain as an optimization — but for Phase 1, the uniform enrich-then-filter path is simpler and proven by the spike. Replace flat filter decomposition in A.collectTransactionResult with: enrich all transactions via Transaction.enrichAll, compile the source's IRFilter tree into a predicate via ir-filter-evaluator, then filter(predicate, enriched). Remove P.isFilterMatch (flat version) and P.isTransactionMatch. Also hoist A.findBenchmarkSecurityId out of per-node loop in collectPositionsResult (call once, pass via context). All existing query-execution-engine.tap.js tests must pass. Run style validator — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: Enrich-then-filter required resolving entity-level Equals('category', X) to Matches on categoryName with hierarchy prefix regex ^X(:|$). Field aliases (category→categoryName, account→accountName, accountType→type) bridge IRFilter field names to enriched/Account properties. Account aliases applied per-predicate-call to avoid polluting Account type validation.

steps.action: Update query-validator.js to validate new IRFilter variants (In, GreaterThan, LessThan, Between, Matches, And, Or, Not). Validate: In field uses same allowlist as Equals or is a known enriched field name; GreaterThan/LessThan/Between values are numbers; Matches pattern is a valid regex (try/catch on new RegExp); And/Or children are non-empty arrays of valid IRFilters; Not child is a valid IRFilter. Recursive depth check. Ensure existing validator tests still pass. Run style validator on changed files — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: As planned. Added collectEntityValueErrors (extracted from inline), collectMatchesErrors (regex validation), extended collectFilterErrors for In entity field validation. Numeric variants (GreaterThan/LessThan/Between) pass through — constructor validates types.

steps.action: Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
steps.note: Simplicity: APPROVE. JS: 1 blocking (if/is vs .match()) — justified because .match() requires all 10 arms with 6 passthrough, and same pattern already approved in build-filter-predicate.js. Non-blocking: case-insensitive regex in category hierarchy match (acceptable — data is consistently cased).

steps.action: git add changed files and commit: 'IRFilter boolean tree — 10 variants, pre-compiled evaluator, enrich-then-filter engine path'
steps.note: Two commits: (1) Fix generator prettier config — objectWrap: collapse mismatch caused dirty regen output. (2) Task work — 13 files, 10 IRFilter variants, pre-compiled evaluator, enrich-then-filter engine path.

steps.action: Create query-ir reducer (store/reducers/query-ir.js). Spike learning: use plain object { [viewId]: Query }, not LookupTable — memoizeReduxStatePerKey works with plain objects via bracket access. Action: SetQueryIR(viewId, query) to store a Query IR per view. Wire into root reducer. Add SetQueryIR to Action type definition and post.js command layer. Grep for all `action.match(` sites to ensure exhaustive coverage. Run `yarn types:generate-all` if Action type definition is generated. Run style validator on all changed files — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: No separate reducer file — inline spread in reducer.js like other simple actions. Action field uses Object (not Query) because generator can't resolve cross-directory type imports. queryIR: {} in initial state.

steps.action: Write TAP test for SetQueryIR round-trip (dispatch → reducer → new state): store Query IR, retrieve by viewId, second SetQueryIR replaces first, reference identity preserved for untouched viewIds. Test file: test/query-ir-reducer.tap.js
steps.note: As planned. 8 tests: store, replace, multi-viewId, reference identity preservation.

steps.action: Create memoized engine selector (store/selectors/query-result.js). Selector takes (state, viewId), reads Query IR from queryIR plain object. If no IR exists for viewId, return undefined. Extract data slices the engine needs (accounts, categories, transactions, securities, lots, prices — NOT full Redux state) and pass as second argument to queryExecutionEngine. Memoize per viewId on IR reference identity AND entity slice identities (if any entity slice changes, cache invalidates). Spike confirmed: memoizeReduxStatePerKey on entity slices + queryIR produces same reference (===) on unrelated state changes and new reference on IR or entity changes. Add to selectors.js barrel export. Run style validator on changed files — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: Inline in selectors.js (matching Categories/Accounts pattern) — no separate file. memoizeReduxStatePerKey on 7 entity keys + queryIR keyed state. Style validator compliant.

steps.action: Spawn jeff-js-reviewer and code-simplicity-reviewer on staged changes. Fix blocking issues.
steps.note: Both APPROVE. JS: 0 blocking (ENGINE_STATE_KEYS conservative, query:'Object' justified per step 10 note). Simplicity: 0 findings, minimal plumbing.

steps.action: Add engine-driven mode to QueryResultPage.jsx. When metadata has a `selector`, use it (existing behavior). When metadata has a `defaultQueryIR` field instead: component reads IR from Redux via useSelector(s => S.QueryIR.forView(s, viewId)), falls back to metadata.defaultQueryIR when undefined, then reads engine result via useSelector(s => S.QueryResult.fromIR(s, ir)). IR only enters Redux when explicitly changed (future: Claude, user edits). Call QueryResult.match() to extract tree data: Identity → tree nodes for DataTable; other variants → throw ('Unsupported QueryResult variant' — fail-fast). Engine-mode metadata uses empty `filters` array (chip UI deferred). Verify each top-level declaration is under its correct section separator. Verify zero non-useSelector hooks in modified file. Run style validator on changed files — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: Decision: modified QueryResult.fromIR selector to accept fallbackIR param and extract tree nodes directly (component gets same shape as selector path). Ternary in useSelector branches on defaultQueryIR presence. prettier-ignore needed for 8-field destructuring.

steps.action: Create seed queries in report-metadata.js. Add ENGINE_TRANSACTION_TREE_METADATA and ENGINE_POSITION_TREE_METADATA constants. Each spreads from its selector-based counterpart (...TRANSACTION_TREE_METADATA) and adds `defaultQueryIR` field with a hardcoded Query IR. Transaction seed: all transactions grouped by category, no filters. Position seed: all positions grouped by account, no filters. Engine-mode constants have empty `filters: []` array. Keep existing selector-based metadata constants (deprecated — to be removed when engine parity confirmed). Also create a SEED_QUERIES constant object with the demo queries from the task's seed_queries field (large_transactions, dining_multi_account, exclude_transfers, payee_pattern, amount_range) — these prove the engine's expressive power beyond selectors. Verify each top-level declaration is under its correct section separator. Run style validator on changed files — verify `isCompliant: true`, fix all violations.
steps.note: T.toQuery helper in Transformers section builds single-source Identity queries. ENGINE_ variants spread from selector-based counterparts. SEED_QUERIES uses all 5 demo filter patterns.

steps.action: Write TAP test for seed query execution: run each query from SEED_QUERIES against fixture data via the engine. Verify: large_transactions returns only transactions with amount > 500; dining_multi_account returns only Food:Dining in specified accounts; exclude_transfers has no Transfer category nodes; payee_pattern matches only WAL* payees; amount_range returns 100-1000 amounts in Food or Shopping. Test file: test/query-language/seed-query-execution.tap.js
steps.note: Decision: queries built inline in test (not imported from report-metadata.js) because report-metadata.js transitively imports .jsx files which TAP can't load. 6 tests, 7 fixture transactions exercising all 5 seed patterns.

verification: CategoryReportPage renders same tree data via engine as it did via S.Transactions.tree selector (unfiltered parity — spike confirmed identical)
InvestmentReportPage renders same position data via engine as it did via S.Positions.tree selector
Seed query 'large_transactions': GreaterThan('amount', 500) returns only high-value transactions — selectors cannot do this
Seed query 'amount_range': And([Between, Or([Equals, Equals])]) filters correctly — compound boolean tree
Seed query 'payee_pattern': Matches('payee', '^WAL') finds regex matches
IRFilter.And([Equals('category', 'Food'), In('account', ['Checking', 'Savings'])]) filters correctly in engine
Query IR persists in Redux per viewId — switching views preserves each view's query
Engine re-execution is memoized — same IR reference returns cached QueryResult; entity data change invalidates cache (spike confirmed)
All existing query-execution-engine.tap.js tests pass with enrich-then-filter path
All existing query-validator.tap.js tests pass with new variant validation
Empty And([]) and Or([]) rejected by both evaluator and validator
Filter tree depth > 20 rejected by evaluator
Invalid regex in Matches throws clear error
